summary: Curate provenanced property values for 13 engineering materials from accessible public sources

rows.name: ASTM A36 structural steel (hot-rolled)
rows.condition: Hot-rolled, as-rolled; strength minimums apply to plates/shapes/bars up to 8 in. thick (plates over 8 in. drop to 32 ksi yield)

properties.source_citation: ASTM A36/A36M, Standard Specification for Carbon Structural Steel — specified minimum yield strength (thickness <= 8 in.). Verified via SSAB ASTM A36 mill datasheet quoting the spec minimum.
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia 'A36 steel' (citing AISC Steel Construction Manual, 8th ed.): minimum yield 36 ksi (250 MPa); also NCEES FE Reference Handbook 9.2, Mechanics of Materials Table 2 (Hibbeler 4th ed.), Structural A36 row: 36 ksi
properties.notes: ASTM spec itself is paywalled; number verified via mill datasheet and two secondary sources quoting the spec minimum. For plates > 8 in. thick the spec minimum is 32 ksi.

properties.source_citation: ASTM A36/A36M — specified tensile strength range 58-80 ksi; 58 ksi recorded as the specified minimum. Verified via SSAB ASTM A36 mill datasheet (tensile strength 58-80 ksi).
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia 'A36 steel': 58-80 ksi (400-550 MPa); NCEES FE Reference Handbook 9.2 Table 2 (Hibbeler), Structural A36 row: ultimate 58 ksi
properties.notes: Spec gives a 58-80 ksi range, not a single value; 58 ksi minimum recorded per task instructions. Mill certs commonly exceed minimums.

properties.source_citation: NCEES FE Reference Handbook 9.2, Mechanics of Materials, 'Table 1 - Typical Material Properties', p. 80: Steel density 0.282 lb/in3 (7.8 Mg/m3)
properties.cross_check: Same handbook Table 2 (Hibbeler, Mechanics of Materials 4th ed.), Structural A36 row: specific weight 0.284 lb/in3; Wikipedia 'A36 steel': 0.28 lb/in3 (7.8 g/cm3), there cited to MatWeb (third-party quote); steelcalculator.app A36 reference: 490 lb/ft3 (7,850 kg/m3)
properties.notes: Generic typical steel value, not an A36 spec quantity (ASTM A36 does not specify density). PDF read directly (pages 76-81 of the handbook's Mechanics of Materials section).

properties.source_citation: NCEES FE Reference Handbook 9.2, Mechanics of Materials, 'Table 1 - Typical Material Properties', p. 80: Steel modulus of elasticity E = 29.0 Mpsi (200.0 GPa)
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia 'A36 steel': 29,000 ksi (200 GPa); steelcalculator.app A36 reference: 29,000 ksi (200,000 MPa); same handbook Table 2 (Hibbeler) A36 row: E = 29.0 x 10^3 ksi
properties.notes: Unit recorded exactly as published in Table 1 ('Mpsi', i.e. 10^6 psi). Typical value for all structural steel grades, not an A36 spec quantity.

properties.source_citation: NCEES FE Reference Handbook 9.2, Mechanics of Materials, 'Table 1 - Typical Material Properties', p. 80: Steel Poisson's ratio v = 0.30
properties.cross_check: steelcalculator.app A36 reference page: Poisson's ratio 0.30
properties.notes: Sources disagree on this one — see discrepancies. 0.30 recorded per FE Reference Handbook Table 1 as instructed.

properties.source_citation: NCEES FE Reference Handbook 9.2, Mechanics of Materials, 'Table 1 - Typical Material Properties', p. 80: Steel modulus of rigidity G = 11.5 Mpsi (80.0 GPa)
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia 'A36 steel': shear modulus 11,500 ksi (79.3 GPa)
properties.notes: Included as a bonus alongside Poisson's ratio. Note AISC-aligned sources use G = 11,200 ksi (see discrepancies).

discrepancies: Poisson's ratio varies by source: 0.30 (FE Reference Handbook 9.2 Table 1, typical steel; also steelcalculator.app), 0.32 (FE Handbook Table 2 / Hibbeler Mechanics of Materials 4th ed., Structural A36 row), 0.26 (Wikipedia A36 article, uncited for this value). 0.30 recorded as the primary value per assignment.

Shear modulus: FE Handbook Table 1 gives 11.5 Mpsi (80.0 GPa) and Wikipedia gives 11,500 ksi, but AISC-style references (steelcalculator.app) use G = 11,200 ksi (77 GPa), and FE Handbook Table 2 (Hibbeler) lists 11.0 x 10^3 ksi for A36.

Density spread is small but real: 0.282 lb/in3 (FE Table 1, generic steel) vs 0.284 lb/in3 (Hibbeler A36 specific weight, FE Table 2) vs 0.28 lb/in3 (Wikipedia, consistent with MatWeb as quoted there) vs 490 lb/ft3 ~ 0.2836 lb/in3 (steelcalculator.app).
Ultimate strength: ASTM spec is a 58-80 ksi range; FE Handbook Table 2 (Hibbeler) lists a single 58 ksi value. Recorded 58 ksi as the spec minimum per instructions.
Yield minimum is thickness-dependent: 36 ksi applies up to 8 in.; plates over 8 in. thick are 32 ksi min (Wikipedia, citing AISC Steel Construction Manual 8th ed.).

rows.name: AISI 1045 medium-carbon steel
rows.condition: Normalized bar (normalised per AS 1442 grade 1045, AISI 1045 equivalent; typical normalizing 840-920 C per supplier datasheets). ALL VALUES TYPICAL, not spec minimums — weakest-citation row. Elastic/physical properties are condition-independent typicals.

properties.source_citation: AZoM, 'AISI 1045 Medium Carbon Steel', Article ID 6130, Physical Properties table: 7.87 g/cc (0.284 lb/in3)
properties.cross_check: MW Components (Elgin) 'Carbon Steel Grade 1045 Fact Sheet' PDF: identical English value 0.284 lb/in3 (metric printed as 7.85 g/cc); Amardeep Steel blog: 7.86 g/cm3
properties.notes: Condition-independent typical. Metric value varies 7.85-7.87 g/cc across sources while the English 0.284 lb/in3 is identical; recorded as published by AZoM. AZoM table content is consistent with values third parties attribute to MatWeb (MatWeb itself not accessed).

properties.source_citation: AZoM, 'AISI 1045 Medium Carbon Steel', Article ID 6130, Mechanical Properties table: Modulus of Elasticity 200 GPa (29000 ksi)
properties.cross_check: Leoscor Alloys, 'AISI 1045 Carbon Steel Round Bar Specifications' PDF, physical properties table: Modulus of Elasticity 29x10^6 psi (= 29,000 ksi, exact match in English units)
properties.notes: Condition-independent typical. MW Components/Elgin sheet gives 206 GPa (29,900 ksi) and Amardeep Steel 209,000 MPa - normal source spread for plain carbon steel; 200 GPa / 29 Msi is the standard handbook typical. See discrepancies.

properties.source_citation: AZoM, 'AISI 1045 Medium Carbon Steel', Article ID 6130, Mechanical Properties table: Poisson's Ratio 0.290
properties.cross_check: MW Components (Elgin) 'Carbon Steel Grade 1045 Fact Sheet' PDF: Poissons Ratio 0.29, annotated 'Typical for steel'
properties.notes: Published as 0.290 by AZoM. One outlier source (Amardeep Steel blog) gives 0.269; not used. Condition-independent.

properties.source_citation: AZoM, 'AISI 1045 Medium Carbon Steel', Article ID 6130, Mechanical Properties table: Shear Modulus 80 GPa (11600 ksi)
properties.cross_check: MW Components (Elgin) 'Carbon Steel Grade 1045 Fact Sheet' PDF: Shear Modulus 80.0 GPa (11600 ksi), annotated 'Estimated from elastic'
properties.notes: Supplementary to Poisson's ratio. Self-consistent with E=200 GPa and nu=0.29 within rounding (E/2(1+nu) = 77.5 GPa).

properties.source_citation: Interlloy Pty Ltd, '1045 Medium Tensile Carbon Steel Bar' datasheet, 'Typical Mechanical Properties - Normalised Condition' table (grade per AS 1442-1992 1045): Yield Strength 410 MPa, Elongation in 50mm 22%, Impact Izod 54 J, Hardness 187 HB / Rc 10
properties.cross_check: Sciencing.com, 'Properties of 1045 Steel': normalized condition yield strength 410 MPa, with matching elongation 22%, Izod 54, 187 HB (independent reproduction of the same dataset)
properties.notes: TYPICAL value for normalized condition, explicitly NOT a guaranteed/spec minimum (Interlloy supplies 1045 to chemistry only). For contrast, ASTM A29-family hot-rolled 1045 estimated minimum yield is 45,000 psi (SAE J1397, verified in Pivot Point reproduction).

properties.source_citation: Interlloy Pty Ltd, '1045 Medium Tensile Carbon Steel Bar' datasheet, 'Typical Mechanical Properties - Normalised Condition' table (grade per AS 1442-1992 1045): Tensile Strength 640 MPa
properties.cross_check: No second source with the exact 640 MPa found. Consistency checks: AZoM/Kloeckner/Leoscor all quote 570-700 MPa typical tensile range for hot-rolled-or-normalised 1045 (640 falls inside); value is consistent with the accompanying 187 HB (~3.4xHB ~ 645 MPa). Sciencing reproduction of the same table prints 540 MPa - suspected transcription typo (see discrepancies).
properties.notes: TYPICAL value for normalized condition, not a spec minimum. Weakest-citation value in this row: single primary supplier datasheet, with third-party reproduction agreeing on every accompanying value except tensile.

discrepancies: Normalized UTS: Interlloy datasheet prints 640 MPa, but third-party reproductions (Sciencing.com; Katalor per search snippet) print 540 MPa with every other value identical (YS 410 MPa, 22% elongation, Izod 54 J, 187 HB). 540 falls outside the 570-700 MPa typical normalized/hot-rolled range quoted by AZoM/Kloeckner/Leoscor and is inconsistent with 187 HB; treated as a propagated 6-to-5 typo and 640 MPa adopted.

The frequently quoted pair 'UTS 625 MPa / YS 530 MPa' for 1045 is the COLD DRAWN 19-32 mm dataset (MW Components/Elgin fact sheet, consistent with values attributed to MatWeb), NOT normalized - easily confused with the normalized row because the UTS happens to be similar.

AZoM's strength table (UTS 565 MPa / YS 310 MPa / 163 HB / 16% elong.) is described as applying to 'black hot-rolled or normalized' supply, but it matches the SAE J1397 hot-rolled estimated values for 1045 (82,000 / 45,000 psi, 163 HB - verified in Pivot Point's reproduction of SAE J1397 Rev. May 1992), i.e. effectively hot-rolled data; therefore not used for normalized strengths.

tubingchina.com's 'Normalized (1650 F): 85,500 / 54,250 psi, 170 HB' appears near a 1045 header but is actually the grade 1040 row - grade 1045 is absent from that table entirely (confirmed by re-fetch). Do not use for 1045.

The ASM-Handbook-style '1045 normalized at 900 C: TS 625 MPa / YS 374 MPa' dataset could not be verified at any legitimately accessible URL during this research (efunda returned HTTP 500; theworldmaterial.com, machinemfg.com, qilusteelgroup.com returned 403; ASTM/ASM are paywalled; MatWeb off-limits per EULA). Not used; honesty over completeness.

Elastic modulus spread across sources: 200 GPa / 29,000 ksi (AZoM, matching Leoscor 29x10^6 psi) vs 206 GPa / 29,900 ksi (MW Components/Elgin) vs 209,000 MPa (Amardeep). 200 GPa adopted as the standard typical.
Poisson's ratio: 0.290 (AZoM, MW Components) vs 0.269 (Amardeep Steel blog, outlier - rejected).
Density: 7.85 vs 7.86 vs 7.87 g/cc across sources; all agree on 0.284 lb/in3 in English units.

rows.name: AISI 4340 low-alloy steel (Ni-Cr-Mo)
rows.condition: Quenched and tempered to Ftu = 260 ksi level per MIL-HDBK-5J Table 2.3.1.0(f1) (AMS 6414, consumable-electrode vacuum-melted; bar, forging, tubing; thickness limits per table footnote: <=1.70 in. martempered / <=2.50 in. oil quench / <=3.50 in. water quench, 200 ft/min flow). Elastic and physical properties are the single nominal values MIL-HDBK-5J lists for all Section 2.3 low-alloy steels (4130/4140/4340/8630 group).

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J, Metallic Materials and Elements for Aerospace Vehicle Structures, 31 January 2003, Table 2.3.1.0(f1), p. 2-22 (omega, lb/in.^3 = 0.283); same value in Tables 2.3.1.0(c1) p. 2-17, (c2) p. 2-18, (g1) p. 2-24, (g2) p. 2-25 for all low-alloy steels
properties.cross_check: Independently hosted MIL-HDBK-5J chapter 2 excerpt (files.engineering.com, 4130Pages_from_MILHBK5J-2.pdf, pp. 2-17/2-18/2-24) shows omega = 0.283 lb/in.^3 for the low-alloy steel group
properties.notes: Handbook lists density as a nominal physical property, not a statistical minimum. Typical commercial datasheets give 7.85 g/cm^3 = 0.284 lb/in^3 (quickparts.com MatWeb-derived sheet; AZoM) - see discrepancies. Page image loaded from the archive.org scan of MIL-HDBK-5J (item milhdbk-5-j, leaf 75 = printed page 2-22).

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J, 31 January 2003, Table 2.3.1.0(f1), p. 2-22: E, 10^3 ksi = 29.0 (tension); Ec, 10^3 ksi = 29.0 (compression); identical in Tables 2.3.1.0(c1), (c2), (g1), (g2)
properties.cross_check: files.engineering.com MIL-HDBK-5J excerpt (Tables 2.3.1.0(c1)/(c2), pp. 2-17/2-18): E = 29.0 x 10^3 ksi; also quickparts.com MatWeb-derived normalized 4340 datasheet: 29,000 ksi (200 GPa)
properties.notes: Published as 29.0 in units of 10^3 ksi (do not double-convert). Moduli in MIL-HDBK-5 tables are nominal/average values, not statistical minimums.

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J, 31 January 2003, Table 2.3.1.0(f1), p. 2-22: mu = 0.32; identical in Tables 2.3.1.0(c1), (c2), (g1), (g2) for all low-alloy steels
properties.cross_check: files.engineering.com MIL-HDBK-5J excerpt (pp. 2-17/2-18/2-24): mu = 0.32
properties.notes: Note: MIL-HDBK-5J's 0.32 disagrees with common typical datasheets (0.29 on the MatWeb-derived quickparts sheet; 0.27-0.30 per AZoM). 0.32 is also slightly inconsistent with the handbook's own E = 29.0 and G = 11.0 Msi (isotropic relation gives mu ~ 0.318). Flagged in discrepancies.

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J, 31 January 2003, Table 2.3.1.0(f1), p. 2-22: G, 10^3 ksi = 11.0; identical in Tables 2.3.1.0(c1), (c2), (g1), (g2)
properties.cross_check: files.engineering.com MIL-HDBK-5J excerpt (pp. 2-17/2-18/2-24): G = 11.0 x 10^3 ksi
properties.notes: MatWeb-derived typical sheet gives 78.0 GPa (11,300 ksi); AZoM gives 80 GPa 'typical for steel'. See discrepancies.

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J, 31 January 2003, Table 2.3.1.0(f1) 'Design Mechanical and Physical Properties of Low-Alloy Steels', p. 2-22: AISI 4340 (AMS 6414, consumable-electrode vacuum-melted), bar/forging/tubing, quenched and tempered, Basis S: Fty = 217 ksi at the Ftu = 260 ksi level (Fcy = 235 ksi, Fsu = 156 ksi, e(L) = 10%)
properties.cross_check: Third-party reproduction of MIL-HDBK-5J Table 2.3.1.0(f) seen in search-result listing (Chegg homework excerpt, gated site not fetched) quotes the same Ftu 260 ksi / Fty 217 ksi pair for AISI 4340
properties.notes: S basis = minimum per the applicable material specification; table footnote b: 'Design values are applicable only to parts for which the indicated Ftu has been substantiated by adequate quality control testing.' Lower Q&T levels (Ftu 125/140/150/160/180/200 ksi with Fty 100/120/132/142/163/176 ksi) appear in Table 2.3.1.0(g2), p. 2-25 (verified at leaf 78 of the same archive.org scan), but per that table's footnote d they carry no statistical (T99/T90) or specification (S) basis.

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J, 31 January 2003, Table 2.3.1.0(f1), p. 2-22: AISI 4340 (AMS 6414, consumable-electrode vacuum-melted), bar/forging/tubing, quenched and tempered, Basis S: Ftu = 260 ksi
properties.cross_check: Chegg search-result listing of MIL-HDBK-5J Table 2.3.1.0(f) quotes Ftu 260 ksi for AISI 4340 (gated site, snippet only); for the normalized condition a MatWeb-derived datasheet (quickparts.com) gives typical UTS 1282 MPa (185,900 psi)
properties.notes: 260 ksi is the classic aerospace heat-treat level for consumable-electrode vacuum-melted 4340 (AMS 6414). Strength is entirely heat-treat dependent; see discrepancies for other levels and typical normalized values. MIL-HDBK-5J page images loaded from the archive.org scan (item milhdbk-5-j): leaf 75 = p. 2-22 (Table 2.3.1.0(f1)), leaf 78 = p. 2-25 (Table 2.3.1.0(g2)).

discrepancies: Poisson's ratio: MIL-HDBK-5J lists mu = 0.32 for all Section 2.3 low-alloy steels, while typical secondary datasheets list 0.29 (MatWeb-derived normalized 4340 sheet hosted at quickparts.com) and AZoM lists 0.27-0.30. 0.32 is also internally inconsistent with the handbook's own E = 29.0 Msi / G = 11.0 Msi (isotropic relation implies ~0.318; with G = 11.3 Msi it implies ~0.28).
Density: MIL-HDBK-5J gives omega = 0.283 lb/in.^3; typical commercial datasheets give 7.85 g/cm^3 = 0.284 lb/in^3 (quickparts/MatWeb-derived, AZoM). Difference is rounding-level (~0.4%).
Shear modulus: MIL-HDBK-5J G = 11.0 x 10^3 ksi vs 78.0 GPa (11,300 ksi) on the MatWeb-derived sheet and 80 GPa (AZoM, 'typical for steel').

Strength values are heat-treat-level dependent and bases differ: the reported Ftu 260 / Fty 217 ksi are S-basis for consumable-electrode vacuum-melted 4340 per AMS 6414 (Table 2.3.1.0(f1), p. 2-22). The general Q&T capability table 2.3.1.0(g2) (p. 2-25, verified) lists Ftu 125/140/150/160/180/200 ksi with Fty 100/120/132/142/163/176 ksi, but its footnote d states there is no statistical (T99/T90) or specification (S) basis for those values. For the normalized condition, a MatWeb-derived typical datasheet (quickparts.com, 25 mm round) gives UTS 1282 MPa (185,900 psi) and YS 862 MPa (125,000 psi) - typical, not minima. AZoM annealed typicals are much lower: 745 MPa UTS / 470 MPa YS.

MIL-HDBK-5J (2003) was cancelled in 2004 and superseded by MMPDS (paywalled); 5J values are quoted here from a freely hosted archive.org scan and an independently hosted chapter-2 excerpt, both loaded and read directly.

rows.name: 6061-T6 aluminum
rows.condition: T6 (and T62), sheet 0.010-0.249 in per AMS 4025/AMS 4027/AMS-QQ-A-250/11 for MIL-HDBK-5J design values; typical entries are generic T6 wrought-product values

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J (31 Jan 2003), Table 3.6.2.0(b1), Design Mechanical and Physical Properties of 6061 Aluminum Alloy Sheet, p. 3-264 (omega, lb/in.3)
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia '6061 aluminium alloy' properties table: 2.70 g/cm3 (consistent)
properties.notes: Read directly from the archive.org page scan of MIL-HDBK-5J (leaf 565 = handbook page 3-264). Physical property, nominal (no statistical basis assigned in 5J).

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J (31 Jan 2003), Table 3.6.2.0(b1), p. 3-264: E, 10^3 ksi = 9.9 (tension); Ec = 10.1 (compression)
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia 6061 properties table: 68 GPa (9,900 ksi); jeelix.com: 68.9 GPa
properties.notes: Tensile modulus. Compressive modulus Ec = 10.1 x 10^3 ksi in same table. Elastic constants in 5J are average (typical) values, not statistical minima. Wikipedia prose rounds to 69 GPa (10,000 ksi).

properties.cross_check: Wikipedia 6061 properties table: 0.33; jeelix.com FEA section: 0.33; modulusmetal.com range 0.325-0.335
properties.notes: Average elastic constant, no statistical basis in 5J.

properties.cross_check: modulusmetal.com (EN AW-6061 T6): shear modulus 25.6-26.9 GPa, consistent with 3.8 x 10^3 ksi
properties.notes: Included alongside Poisson ratio for completeness; average elastic constant.

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J (31 Jan 2003), Table 3.6.2.0(b1), p. 3-264: Fty, A basis, L direction, T6/T62 sheet 0.010-0.249 in
properties.cross_check: ASTM B209 specified minimum yield 35 ksi (240 MPa) as quoted by Wikipedia '6061 aluminium alloy'; matches the table's LT A-basis value of 35 ksi
properties.notes: A-basis (99% exceedance, 95% confidence) design minimum. Direction-dependent: L = 36 ksi, LT = 35 ksi (A basis); B basis L = 38, LT = 37 ksi. Read directly from archive.org scan of the 5J table.

properties.source_citation: Jeelix, '6061-T6 Aluminum Yield Strength' engineering guide: typical design value 276 MPa (40 ksi); consistent with ASM-handbook-derived typical values widely quoted (and consistent with MatWeb)
properties.cross_check: machinemfg.com and hardwarecustom.com guides (via search snippets): 276 MPa (40,000 psi) at 0.2% offset; Wikipedia gives typical 270 MPa (39 ksi) - see discrepancies
properties.notes: Typical (average) value, not a guaranteed minimum. Same page states the ASTM guaranteed minimum as 240 MPa (35 ksi).

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J (31 Jan 2003), Table 3.6.2.0(b1), p. 3-264: Ftu, A basis, L and LT directions, T6/T62 sheet 0.010-0.249 in
properties.cross_check: ASTM B209 specified minimum tensile strength 42 ksi (290 MPa) as quoted by Wikipedia '6061 aluminium alloy'
properties.notes: A-basis design minimum; 42 ksi in both L and LT directions (B basis = 43 ksi). Read directly from archive.org scan of the 5J table.

properties.source_citation: Jeelix, '6061-T6 Aluminum Yield Strength' engineering guide, Section 5.2: typical ultimate tensile strength 310 MPa; consistent with ASM-handbook-derived typical values (and consistent with MatWeb)
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia '6061 aluminium alloy': 'more typical values are 310 MPa (45 ksi)'
properties.notes: Typical (average) value; ASTM B209 / 5J spec-level minimum is 42 ksi (290 MPa).

discrepancies: Typical yield strength: most ASM-derived sources give 276 MPa (40 ksi), but Wikipedia's prose gives 270 MPa (39 ksi) citing MakeItFrom; both are 'typical' with no statistical basis - we recorded 276 MPa per the assignment, flagging the 270 MPa variant.

Young's modulus: Wikipedia prose says 69 GPa (10,000 ksi) 'regardless of temper', while its own table and MIL-HDBK-5J give 68 GPa / 9.9 x 10^3 ksi (tension); 5J lists compression modulus separately as 10.1 x 10^3 ksi.

MIL-HDBK-5J A-basis Fty is direction-dependent for T6 sheet: 36 ksi (L) vs 35 ksi (LT); the LT value coincides with the ASTM B209 spec minimum of 35 ksi. We recorded the L value (36 ksi) with the LT value noted.
MatWeb was not accessed per EULA restriction; 'consistent with MatWeb' noted only via third-party quotations of the same ASM typical values.

rows.name: 7075-T6 aluminum
rows.condition: T6 temper; design-minimum entries are bare sheet per QQ-A-250/12 (0.040-0.249 in, MIL-HDBK-5 lineage); typical entries are T6/T651 nominal values

properties.source_citation: NASA CR-123773, Materials Data Handbook: Aluminum Alloy 7075 (2nd Ed., April 1972), Sec. 9.1: density 0.101 lb/in3 at 68 F (Sec. 9.11 gives specific gravity 2.80 g/cm3 at 20 C). This is the MIL-HDBK-5 lineage value; MIL-HDBK-5J Table 3.7.6.0(b1) lists the same omega = 0.101 lb/in3.
properties.cross_check: Gabrian 7075 datasheet and Wikipedia both list 2.81 g/cm3 = 0.102 lb/in3 (rounding-level difference, see discrepancies)
properties.notes: MIL-HDBK-5J itself could not be fetched (68.7 MB > fetch limit); the 0.101 lb/in3 value was verified in NASA CR-123773, which republishes MIL-HDBK-5 data. Nominal physical property, no statistical basis.

properties.source_citation: NASA CR-123773, Sec. 7.221: 'Design value of E. All products and tempers: E = 10.3 x 10^3 ksi', citing Ref. 7.5 = MIL-HDBK-5A (Feb 1966, change order Jan 1970). MIL-HDBK-5J Sec. 3.7.6 lists the identical E = 10.3 x 10^3 ksi for 7075-T6 sheet.
properties.cross_check: Gabrian 7075 datasheet and Wikipedia: typical E = 10,400 ksi (71.7 GPa) - agrees within 1% (typical vs design value)
properties.notes: Handbook design (room-temperature tension) value, not a statistical A/B minimum; design_minimum is the closest basis category. NASA Sec. 7.231 also gives compression modulus Ec = 10.5 x 10^3 ksi.

properties.source_citation: NASA CR-123773, Sec. 7.21: 'Poisson's ratio, 0.33', citing Metals Handbook Vol. 1, 8th Ed., ASM 1961. Same mu = 0.33 is listed in MIL-HDBK-5J Table 3.7.6.0(b1).
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia '7075 aluminium alloy' properties table: Poisson's ratio 0.33

properties.source_citation: NASA CR-123773, Sec. 7.241: 'Design value of G. 3.9 x 10^3 ksi', citing Ref. 7.5 = MIL-HDBK-5A. MIL-HDBK-5J Table 3.7.6.0(b1) lists the same G = 3.9 x 10^3 ksi for 7075-T6 sheet.
properties.cross_check: none found online beyond consistency with widely quoted typical 26.9 GPa (~3.9 Msi); also consistent with E=10.3 Msi and mu=0.33 via G=E/2(1+mu)=3.87 Msi
properties.notes: Handbook design value (modulus of rigidity), not statistical.

properties.source_citation: NASA CR-123773, Table 7.4112 'Design Tensile Properties for T6 and T651 Sheet and Plate', QQ-A-250/12 (7075), T6 bare sheet 0.040-0.249 in, A basis, L direction: Fty = 67 ksi (LT = 66 ksi; B basis L = 70 ksi). Republished from MIL-HDBK-5A (Ref. 7.5).
properties.cross_check: Aero Metals Alliance QQ-A-250/12 T6 sheet datasheet: spec minimum proof strength 469 MPa (= 68 ksi) for 1.0-3.17 mm (0.040-0.125 in), LT; Alcoa 7075 datasheet: T6 sheet LT yield min 63-69 ksi (434-476 MPa) over 0.008-0.249 in
properties.notes: A-basis statistical minimum. Assignment preferred MIL-HDBK-5J Table 3.7.6.0(b1) sheet values, but the 5J PDF (68.7 MB) exceeded fetch limits; the verified MIL-HDBK-5A vintage A value is reported instead and the current spec minimum (68 ksi LT for 0.040-0.125 in) is given as cross-check.

properties.source_citation: NASA CR-123773, Table 7.4112, QQ-A-250/12 (7075), T6 bare sheet 0.040-0.249 in, A basis, L direction: Ftu = 77 ksi (LT = 77 ksi; B basis = 79 ksi). Republished from MIL-HDBK-5A (Ref. 7.5).
properties.cross_check: Alcoa 7075 datasheet (hosted by CALM Aluminium): T6 sheet 0.008-0.249 in tensile min 74-78 ksi (510-538 MPa) LT; Aero Metals Alliance QQ-A-250/12: 538 MPa (= 78 ksi) min for 1.0-6.3 mm
properties.notes: A-basis statistical minimum, 5A vintage. Current AMS-QQ-A-250/12 spec minimum for 0.040-0.125 in sheet is 78 ksi LT (see discrepancies).

properties.source_citation: Gabrian International, '7075 Aluminum Alloy: Properties' datasheet, Mechanical Properties table, 7075-T6/-T651: Yield Strength 503 MPa | 73000 psi.
properties.cross_check: NASA CR-123773 Tabular Abstract: typical Fty (T6, T651) = 73.0 ksi; consistent with MatWeb-style typicals as quoted by third parties (503 MPa)
properties.notes: Typical/nominal value, no statistical significance.

properties.source_citation: Gabrian International, '7075 Aluminum Alloy: Properties' datasheet, Mechanical Properties table, 7075-T6/-T651: Tensile Strength 572 MPa | 83000 psi.
properties.cross_check: NASA CR-123773 Tabular Abstract: typical Ftu (T6, T651) = 83.0 ksi; Wikipedia: tensile strength 572 MPa (83.0 ksi)
properties.notes: Typical/nominal value, no statistical significance.

discrepancies: MIL-HDBK-5J Table 3.7.6.0(b1) could not be verified directly: the only accessible copies (Abbott Aerospace, archive.org, everyspec) are single ~67-70 MB PDFs exceeding the 10 MB fetch limit, archive.org search-inside returned 'No hOCR or Abbyy file present', and the Purdue excerpt returned HTTP 401. Design values reported are the MIL-HDBK-5A vintage republished verbatim in NASA CR-123773 (Ref. 7.5 confirmed as MIL-HDBK-5A); the elastic/physical constants (E 10.3 Msi, G 3.9 Msi, mu 0.33, omega 0.101 lb/in3) are unchanged in 5J, but 5J restructures sheet thickness ranges and its A-basis tensile values for 0.040-0.125 in bare sheet are slightly higher than the 5A values reported here.

Ultimate/yield design minimums: 5A-era A-basis Ftu = 77 ksi (0.040-0.249 in) vs current AMS-QQ-A-250/12 spec minimum Ftu = 78 ksi LT and Fty = 68 ksi LT for 0.040-0.125 in (verified via Alcoa 74-78/63-69 ksi ranges and Aero Metals Alliance 538/469 MPa). About a 1 ksi generation gap.

Density: MIL-HDBK-5 lineage gives 0.101 lb/in3 / 2.80 g/cm3 (NASA CR-123773), while Aluminum Association/ASM-style typicals (Gabrian, Wikipedia) give 2.81 g/cm3 / 0.102 lb/in3 - ~1% rounding-level disagreement; pipeline should pick one convention.
Young's modulus: design value 10.3 x 10^3 ksi vs typical 10.4 x 10^3 ksi (71.7 GPa) - basis difference (design vs typical), not a true conflict.

Aero Metals Alliance datasheet lists density 2.71 g/cm3, clearly erroneous vs the 2.80-2.81 g/cm3 consensus; its strength minimums nevertheless match QQ-A-250/12 and were used only for strength cross-checks.
MatWeb was not accessed per EULA restriction; 'consistent with MatWeb' noted only where third parties quote the same 572/503 MPa typicals.

rows.name: 2024-T3 aluminum sheet (bare)
rows.condition: T3, bare flat sheet 0.010-0.128 in. thick, AMS 4037 / AMS-QQ-A-250/4 (MIL-HDBK-5J Table 3.2.3.0(b1), p. 3-71)

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J (31 Jan 2003), Table 3.2.3.0(b1), Design Mechanical and Physical Properties of 2024 Aluminum Alloy Sheet and Plate, p. 3-71 (omega, lb/in.3 = 0.100)
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia '2024 aluminium alloy' gives 2.78 g/cm3 (0.1 lb/in3); Juvinall Appendix F reprint of Table 3.2.3.0(b1) also shows 0.100 lb/in3

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J (31 Jan 2003), Table 3.2.3.0(b1), p. 3-71: E, 10^3 ksi = 10.5 for T3 sheet (Ec = 10.7 in compression)
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia '2024 aluminium alloy': Young's modulus 73 GPa (10.6 Msi) typical across tempers
properties.notes: Tension modulus for the sheet columns of the table; compression modulus Ec = 10.7 x 10^3 ksi. The T351 plate columns of the same table list E = 10.7 / Ec = 10.9. Moduli in MIL-HDBK-5 are average values, not A/B-basis minima.

properties.cross_check: Juvinall & Marshek, Fundamentals of Machine Component Design, Appendix F (reprint of MIL-HDBK-5J Table 3.2.3.0(b1)): mu = 0.33 (verified at https://www.cittastudi.it/repository/3281/Juvinall%20%20Appendice%20F_Metalic%20Materials%20and%20Elements%20for%20Aerospace%20Vehicle%20Structures.pdf)
properties.notes: Average value; applies to all sheet and plate columns of Table 3.2.3.0(b1).

properties.cross_check: Juvinall Appendix F reprint of MIL-HDBK-5J Table 3.2.3.0(b1): G = 4.0 x 10^3 ksi
properties.notes: Average value. Provided in addition to Poisson's ratio; consistent with E/(2(1+mu)) using E = 10.5-10.7 Msi.

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J (31 Jan 2003), Table 3.2.3.0(b1), p. 3-71: Fty (tension yield), A basis, L direction = 47 ksi for T3 sheet 0.010-0.128 in. (LT = 42 ksi; B basis: L = 48, LT = 43)
properties.cross_check: stressebook.com (quoting MMPDS Table 3.2.4.0(b1), same data lineage, AMS 4037 sheet 0.010-0.128): Fty = 42 ksi (LT, A-basis) - matches the LT entry of this column (https://www.stressebook.com/angle-bracket-sizing-stress-analysis/)
properties.notes: A-basis (99% exceedance, 95% confidence) statistical design minimum, longitudinal (L) grain direction. Grain-direction dependent: LT A-basis = 42 ksi. Typical (average) yield is ~50 ksi per United Aluminum datasheet. Educational reference only, not for design.

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J (31 Jan 2003), Table 3.2.3.0(b1), p. 3-71: Ftu (tension ultimate), A basis, L direction = 64 ksi for T3 sheet 0.010-0.128 in. (LT = 63 ksi; B basis: L = 65, LT = 64)
properties.cross_check: stressebook.com (quoting MMPDS Table 3.2.4.0(b1), AMS 4037 sheet 0.010-0.128): Ftu = 63 ksi (LT, lowest grain direction, A-basis) - matches the LT entry of this column (https://www.stressebook.com/angle-bracket-sizing-stress-analysis/)
properties.notes: A-basis statistical design minimum, longitudinal (L) grain direction; LT A-basis = 63 ksi. Typical (average) UTS is ~70 ksi per United Aluminum datasheet (https://unitedaluminum.com/2024-aluminum-alloy/). Educational reference only, not for design.

properties.source_citation: United Aluminum, '2024 Aluminum Alloy' datasheet, typical mechanical properties for T3 temper: Tensile Strength 70 ksi, Yield 50 ksi, Elongation 16% (averages, explicitly 'not guaranteed' and 'should not be used for design purposes')
properties.cross_check: Consistent with the typical 2024-T3 values widely attributed to ASM/MatWeb by third parties (UTS 483 MPa / 70 ksi, YS 345 MPa / 50 ksi); MatWeb itself not accessed per EULA
properties.notes: Typical (average) cross-check row, included alongside the MIL-HDBK-5J A-basis design minimum to show the spread between typical and statistical-minimum values.

discrepancies: Wikipedia's 2024-T3 'typical' strengths (UTS 400-430 MPa / 58-62 ksi, yield 270-280 MPa / 39-40 ksi) are well below both the MIL-HDBK-5J A-basis minima (Ftu 63-64 ksi, Fty 42-47 ksi) and United Aluminum's typical values (70 ksi / 50 ksi); they appear to be Alclad (clad) thin-sheet specification minimums mislabeled as typical T3 values. The handbook and supplier values were preferred.

Tension modulus differs slightly by source/product form: MIL-HDBK-5J lists E = 10.5 x 10^3 ksi for T3 sheet (Ec = 10.7) but E = 10.7 x 10^3 ksi for T351 plate in the same table; the commonly quoted typical value is 10.6 Msi (73 GPa, Wikipedia). Recorded the sheet-column value 10.5.

Newer MMPDS editions renumber the 2024 table to 3.2.4.0(b1) (MIL-HDBK-5J uses 3.2.3.0(b1)); the values quoted from MMPDS by stressebook.com (Ftu 63 ksi LT, Fty 42 ksi LT, A-basis) match the 5J table exactly.
Yield and ultimate strengths are strongly grain-direction dependent (L vs LT differ by 1-5 ksi); the single recorded value is the L direction with LT noted in the property notes.

rows.name: Ti-6Al-4V
rows.condition: Annealed; primary strength values are A-basis for sheet <= 0.1875 in per AMS 4911 / AMS-T-9046 Comp. AB-1 (MIL-HDBK-5J Table 5.4.1.0(b)); elastic/physical values from same table (sheet, strip, and plate)

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J, Metallic Materials and Elements for Aerospace Vehicle Structures, 31 Jan 2003, Table 5.4.1.0(b), p. 5-53 (omega, lb/in.^3) — read from Internet Archive scan (item milhdbk-5-j, leaf 944)
properties.cross_check: Rolled Alloys 6Al-4V Data Sheet (Bulletin 1052USe): Density 0.160 lb/inch3; also United Performance Metals datasheet: 0.16 lb/in3
properties.notes: Published without statistical basis (nominal physical property). Stable citation page: https://archive.org/details/milhdbk-5-j. Wikipedia gives 4.429-4.512 g/cm3 (0.160-0.163 lb/cu in), consistent.

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J, 31 Jan 2003, Table 5.4.1.0(b), p. 5-53: E, 10^3 ksi = 16.0 (annealed Ti-6Al-4V sheet, strip, and plate; tension) — read from Internet Archive scan (item milhdbk-5-j, leaf 944)
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia Ti-6Al-4V property table (citing Carpenter/AZoM): Young's modulus 104-113 GPa (15.1-16.4 x10^6 psi), which brackets 16.0; United Performance Metals datasheet: Modulus of Elasticity Tension 16.5 (x10^6 psi)
properties.notes: Tension modulus; same table gives compression modulus Ec = 16.4 x10^3 ksi. MIL-HDBK-5J Table 5.4.1.0(c1), p. 5-54 (annealed AMS 4928 bar) gives E = 16.9 x10^3 ksi and Ec = 17.2 x10^3 ksi — the 16.0 vs 16.9 Msi spread is product-form dependent within the same handbook. Elastic moduli in MIL-HDBK-5 carry no A/B/S statistical basis (treated as typical/average).

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J, 31 Jan 2003, Table 5.4.1.0(b), p. 5-53: mu = 0.31 (annealed Ti-6Al-4V sheet, strip, and plate) — read from Internet Archive scan (item milhdbk-5-j, leaf 944)
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia Ti-6Al-4V property table: Poisson's ratio 0.31-0.37 (lower bound matches 0.31)
properties.notes: Bar table 5.4.1.0(c1) lists the same mu = 0.31. Some typical-property compilations quote ~0.342 (consistent with MatWeb-style typical data as quoted by third parties); MIL-HDBK-5J uses 0.31.

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J, 31 Jan 2003, Table 5.4.1.0(b), p. 5-53: G, 10^3 ksi = 6.2 (annealed Ti-6Al-4V sheet, strip, and plate) — read from Internet Archive scan (item milhdbk-5-j, leaf 944)
properties.cross_check: Wikipedia Ti-6Al-4V property table: shear modulus 40-45 GPa (5.8-6.5 x10^6 psi), which brackets 6.2
properties.notes: Same G = 6.2 x10^3 ksi listed in bar table 5.4.1.0(c1), p. 5-54.

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J, 31 Jan 2003, Table 5.4.1.0(b), p. 5-53: Fty(L) = 126 ksi, A-basis, annealed Ti-6Al-4V sheet <= 0.1875 in (AMS 4911 / AMS-T-9046 Comp. AB-1) — read from Internet Archive scan (item milhdbk-5-j, leaf 944)
properties.cross_check: Rolled Alloys 6Al-4V Data Sheet, 'Specified, AMS 4911, annealed sheet & plate': 0.2% Yield Strength 126 ksi for sizes up to 0.1874 in (spec minimum, same number)
properties.notes: A-basis (statistical design minimum). B-basis is 131 ksi; LT direction same as L (126/131). Plate 0.1875-2.000 in: A-basis Fty 120 ksi. Annealed AMS 4928 bar (Table 5.4.1.0(c1), p. 5-54): S-basis Fty(L) 125 ksi for <0.500 in; AMS 4928 spec minimum yield is 120 ksi (Upmet datasheet lists Yield Strength 120000 psi).

properties.source_citation: MIL-HDBK-5J, 31 Jan 2003, Table 5.4.1.0(b), p. 5-53: Ftu(L) = 134 ksi, A-basis, annealed Ti-6Al-4V sheet <= 0.1875 in (AMS 4911 / AMS-T-9046 Comp. AB-1) — read from Internet Archive scan (item milhdbk-5-j, leaf 944)
properties.cross_check: Rolled Alloys 6Al-4V Data Sheet, 'Specified, AMS 4911, annealed sheet & plate': Tensile Strength 134 ksi for sizes up to 0.1874 in (spec minimum, same number)
properties.notes: A-basis (statistical design minimum). B-basis is 139 ksi. Plate 0.1875-2.000 in: A-basis Ftu 130 ksi. Annealed AMS 4928 bar (Table 5.4.1.0(c1), p. 5-54): S-basis Ftu(L) 135 ksi for <0.500 in; Upmet datasheet lists UTS 131000 psi for annealed bar. Rolled Alloys typical annealed bar range 138-155 ksi.

discrepancies: Young's modulus differs by product form within MIL-HDBK-5J itself: E = 16.0 x10^3 ksi for annealed sheet/strip/plate (Table 5.4.1.0(b)) vs E = 16.9 x10^3 ksi for annealed AMS 4928 bar (Table 5.4.1.0(c1)). Supplier typicals fall in between (Upmet 16.5 x10^6 psi tension; Rolled Alloys 16.7 x10^6 psi at 70 F, STA condition), while Wikipedia's quoted range tops out at 113 GPa (16.4 x10^6 psi), slightly below the 5J bar value. Recorded 16.0 (sheet table) as primary per assignment; both values noted.

Strength minimums depend strongly on product form and thickness: sheet <=0.1875 in A-basis 134/126 ksi vs plate 0.1875-2.000 in A-basis 130/120 ksi vs AMS 4928 bar S-basis 135/125 ksi (<0.500 in), while the AMS 4928 purchase-spec minimum is ~130/120 ksi (Upmet quotes 131/120 ksi). A single 'annealed Ti-6Al-4V strength' is therefore ambiguous without form/thickness.

Wikipedia's typical tensile range (yield 880-920 MPa / 128-133 ksi) sits slightly above the 5J sheet A-basis 126 ksi — expected, since A-basis design minima are statistically conservative relative to typical test values.
Poisson's ratio: MIL-HDBK-5J uses 0.31; typical-property compilations often quote up to ~0.342 (Wikipedia range 0.31-0.37). Recorded the handbook value.

rows.name: 304 stainless steel
rows.condition: Annealed (ASTM A240 Condition A, sheet/strip/plate); physical properties at room temperature

properties.source_citation: ASTM A240/A240M, Type 304 (UNS S30400), specified minimum 0.2% offset yield strength, annealed; spec is paywalled so value verified via Penn Stainless 'Stainless Steel Plate 304/304L ASTM A240' product page quoting the A240 minimums ('minimum yield strength at 0.2% of 30 ksi')
properties.cross_check: Genn-Hann Stainless ASTM A240 explainer (https://www.ghinox.com/astm-a240/): 'Type 304: ... yield strength of 205 MPa (30 ksi)'
properties.notes: ASTM spec itself not accessed (paywalled); number confirmed on two independent supplier pages quoting the spec. Typical annealed yield is much higher (AK Steel datasheet lists 42 ksi typical for 304). Note: dual-certified 304/304L products are often listed against the 304L minimum of 25 ksi.

properties.source_citation: ASTM A240/A240M, Type 304 (UNS S30400), specified minimum tensile strength, annealed; verified via Penn Stainless 'Stainless Steel Plate 304/304L ASTM A240' product page quoting the A240 minimums ('minimum tensile strength of 75 ksi')
properties.cross_check: Genn-Hann Stainless ASTM A240 explainer (https://www.ghinox.com/astm-a240/): 'Type 304: minimum tensile strength of 515 MPa (75 ksi)'
properties.notes: ASTM spec itself not accessed (paywalled). Typical annealed UTS is higher (AK Steel lists 90 ksi typical). Rolled Alloys' dual-cert 304/304L datasheet lists 70 ksi, which is the Type 304L minimum - see discrepancies.

properties.source_citation: Rolled Alloys, '304/304L Data Sheet' (rev. 08/23), Physical Properties table: 'Density 0.285 lb/in3'
properties.cross_check: Penn Stainless 304/304L ASTM A240 plate page lists '0.285 lbm/in3 at 68 F'
properties.notes: AK Steel datasheet rounds to 0.29 lb/in3 (8.03 g/cm3); kmac-distribution (ASM-style AISI Type 304 data) lists 0.289 lb/in3. 0.285 lb/in3 corresponds to ~7.9 g/cm3 figure widely used for 304.

properties.source_citation: AK Steel Corporation, '304/304L Stainless Steel Product Data Sheet', doc. 304/304L-S-8-01-07 (7/07), Physical Properties: 'Modulus of Elasticity, ksi (MPa): 28.0 x 10^3 (193 x 10^3) in tension'
properties.cross_check: Penn Stainless 304/304L ASTM A240 plate page lists Modulus of Elasticity '28 psi x 10^6'
properties.notes: Equivalent to 28.0 x 10^6 psi (193 GPa), tension, room temperature. Rolled Alloys datasheet lists 29 x 10^6 psi at 68 F; kmac (ASM-style) gives 28000-29000 ksi range - see discrepancies. PDF verified via spacematdb.com mirror of the AK Steel datasheet.

properties.source_citation: K-mac Distribution, '304 Stainless Material Technical Data Sheet' (AISI Type 304, ASM-style typical property listing): Poisson's Ratio 0.29
properties.cross_check: Rolled Alloys 304/304L Data Sheet (rev. 08/23) lists 'Poisson Ratio 0.3' (consistent at one significant figure)
properties.notes: Third-party page republishing the widely quoted ASM/AISI Type 304 typical dataset (consistent with MatWeb-quoted values; MatWeb itself NOT accessed per policy). AZoM gives a generic 0.265-0.275 range for S30400 - see discrepancies. 0.29 is the most commonly cited typical value for annealed 304.

properties.source_citation: AK Steel Corporation, '304/304L Stainless Steel Product Data Sheet', doc. 304/304L-S-8-01-07 (7/07), Physical Properties: 'Modulus of Elasticity, ksi (MPa): ... 11.2 x 10^3 (78 x 10^3) in torsion'
properties.cross_check: AZoM S30400 property page gives shear modulus 74-81 GPa (10.7-11.7 x 10^6 psi), bracketing 78 GPa; kmac-distribution lists 12500 ksi (disagrees - see discrepancies)
properties.notes: Published by AK Steel as modulus of elasticity 'in torsion'. Supplementary to Poisson's ratio; sources disagree (11.2 vs 12.5 x 10^3 ksi), flagged in discrepancies.

discrepancies: Rolled Alloys' dual-certified '304/304L' datasheet lists ASTM A240 minimums of UTS 70 ksi / 0.2% YS 25 ksi - those are the Type 304L minimums, not Type 304's 75/30 ksi. Penn Stainless and Genn-Hann both confirm 75 ksi / 30 ksi for straight Type 304; dual-cert product literature commonly quotes the lower L-grade floor.

Young's modulus: AK Steel and Penn Stainless give 28 x 10^6 psi (193 GPa); Rolled Alloys lists 29 x 10^6 psi at 68 F; kmac (ASM-style) gives a 28000-29000 ksi range. 28 x 10^6 psi (193 GPa) recorded as the most commonly published typical value.
Poisson's ratio: 0.29 (kmac/ASM-style typical) vs 0.3 (Rolled Alloys, likely rounded) vs 0.265-0.275 (AZoM generic S30400 range).
Density: 0.285 lb/in3 (Rolled Alloys, Penn Stainless) vs 0.289 lb/in3 (kmac) vs 0.29 lb/in3 / 8.03 g/cm3 (AK Steel) - roughly 7.9-8.03 g/cm3 spread, typical for austenitic grade literature.

Shear modulus: AK Steel lists 11.2 x 10^3 ksi (78 GPa) 'in torsion' while kmac lists 12500 ksi (86 GPa). Note AK Steel's own E=28.0e3 ksi with G=11.2e3 ksi implies nu ~ 0.25, internally inconsistent with the commonly cited nu = 0.29.
MIL-HDBK-5J not used: assignment specified ASTM A240 minimums for strength and typical elastic/physical properties; no 5J table consulted for this row.

rows.name: Gray Cast Iron, ASTM A48 Class 30
rows.condition: As-cast, classified by separately cast test bars per ASTM A48/A48M (Class 30 = 30 ksi min tensile)

properties.source_citation: ASTM A48/A48M, Standard Specification for Gray Iron Castings, Class 30: specified minimum tensile strength 30 ksi [207 MPa] (paywalled; number verified via foundry datasheets quoting the spec)
properties.cross_check: iron-foundry.com ASTM A48 Class 30 page: 'min. tensile strength 207 MPa' (https://www.iron-foundry.com/astm-a48-class-30.html); also castingsr.com guide: 207 MPa (30,000 psi)
properties.notes: Class number equals the spec minimum tensile strength in ksi. Gray cast iron is brittle with a strongly nonlinear stress-strain curve and exhibits NO meaningful yield point, so yield_strength is intentionally omitted (castingsr.com guide explicitly notes no distinct yield behavior is specified for this material).

properties.source_citation: Penticton Foundry, Gray Iron ASTM A48 Class 30 Data Sheet: compressive strength 109 ksi (752 MPa)
properties.cross_check: tubingchina.com 'Mechanical Properties of Gray Cast Iron' class table (reproducing the classic ASTM-class typical-property table, cf. Shigley Table A-24): Class 30 compressive strength 109 kpsi (https://tubingchina.com/Mechanical-Properties-of-Gray-Cast-Iron.htm)
properties.notes: Typical, not a spec minimum (ASTM A48 specifies tensile only). About 3.6x the tensile class minimum, characteristic of gray iron.

properties.source_citation: Davis, J.R. (ed.), Metals Handbook Desk Edition, ASM International, 1998, as quoted by AmesWeb 'Young's Modulus of Cast Iron': ASTM A48 Class 30 = 13.0-16.4 x 10^6 psi (90-113 GPa), secant modulus in tension
properties.cross_check: tubingchina.com gray cast iron class table: Class 30 modulus of elasticity (tension) 13.0-16.4 Mpsi; castingsr.com A48 Class 30 guide: 90-113 GPa (13-16.5 x 10^6 psi)
properties.notes: RANGE: published as 13.0-16.4 x 10^6 psi; value recorded is the lower bound of the range, upper bound 16.4. Gray iron does not obey Hooke's law; this is the SECANT modulus, defined as the slope from the origin to 25% of tensile strength on the stress-strain curve. Store as a range, not a point value.

properties.source_citation: AmesWeb 'Poisson's Ratio of Cast Iron' (citing Callister 2007 and Davis, ASM Metals Handbook Desk Edition 1998): gray irons ~0.26 (grades G1800/G3000/G4000 all listed 0.26)
properties.cross_check: Sonelastic ferrous-metals property table (sourced from ASM Handbooks Vol. 1 & 2): gray iron grades G1800 and G4000 Poisson's ratio 0.26 (https://www.sonelastic.com/en/fundamentals/tables-of-materials-properties/ferrous-metals.html)
properties.notes: Generic gray-iron value, not class-30-specific; castingsr.com quotes 0.26-0.28 for Class 30. Given the nonlinear elasticity, treat as nominal.

properties.source_citation: Penticton Foundry, Gray Iron ASTM A48 Class 30 Data Sheet: density 0.258 lb/in3 (7.15 g/cm3)
properties.cross_check: castingsr.com A48 Class 30 guide: density 7.10-7.25 g/cm3, which brackets 7.15 g/cm3 (= 0.258 lb/in3) (https://castingsr.com/technical-knowledge-of-astm-a48-class-30-a-complete-guide/)
properties.notes: Gray iron density varies slightly with graphite content/section size; 0.258 lb/in3 is the commonly published class 30 nominal.

discrepancies: Yield strength: no value reported anywhere because gray cast iron has no meaningful yield point (brittle, nonlinear elasticity); any 'yield' figures occasionally seen for gray iron are estimates and were deliberately excluded.

Young's modulus: some aggregator sites surfaced in search (e.g. MakeItFrom for ASTM Grade 30, echoed by nuclear-power.com) list E around 180 GPa (26 x 10^6 psi), which conflicts sharply with the ASM Metals Handbook secant range of 13.0-16.4 x 10^6 psi (90-113 GPa) used here; the ~26 Msi figure appears erroneous for class 30 gray iron and was rejected.

Tensile strength: tubingchina's typical-property table lists 31 kpsi typical for Class 30 vs the 30 ksi ASTM A48 spec minimum recorded here - consistent (typical slightly above minimum), basis difference only.
Hardness ranges differ between sources (Penticton 174-210 BHN vs iron-foundry/castingsr 187-241 BHN) - not a requested property, noted for completeness.
Poisson's ratio: castingsr.com gives 0.26-0.28 for Class 30 vs the ASM-sourced 0.26 recorded; minor spread, 0.26 retained as the handbook nominal.

MatWeb was never accessed per EULA restriction; all values verified via foundry datasheets, AmesWeb (quoting ASM Metals Handbook Desk Edition), Sonelastic (quoting ASM Handbooks), and supplier technical guides.

properties.notes: Yield basis is 0.5% extension under load, not 0.2% offset. The CDA page was loaded but its H02 table row rendered with the yield cell dropped by the HTML-to-text conversion (remaining cells 62/25/70/65/40/18 match TS/El/HRB/HR30T/shear/fatigue only with YS=52); the 52 ksi value was directly seen on MakeItFrom as 360 MPa (52 x 10^3 psi). For comparison, ASTM B927/B927M-23 spec MINIMUM for H02 rod under 1/2 in dia is 35 ksi (Wieland Concast datasheet) — different product form and basis.

properties.source_citation: Copper Development Association, C26000 Alloy datasheet (alloys.copper.org), Mechanical Properties, Flat Products, H02 (1/2 Hard) temper, 0.040 in section: tensile strength 62 ksi Typ
properties.cross_check: Copper and Brass Sales (materialsplus.com) C26000 H02 sheet listings: 62 ksi tensile (via search results); MakeItFrom H02 page: 420 MPa (61 x 10^3 psi)
properties.notes: Typical, not a spec minimum. ASTM B927/B927M-23 spec MINIMUM for H02 rod under 1/2 in dia is 57 ksi (Wieland Concast datasheet) — different product form.

discrepancies: Elastic modulus: AZoM lists 117 GPa (16969 ksi) while CDA, Wieland Concast (quoting CDA), and MakeItFrom all give 16000 ksi (110 GPa). The CDA value 16000 ksi was used; AZoM appears to be the outlier.
Poisson's ratio: AZoM 0.34 vs MakeItFrom 0.31 vs 0.33 implied by CDA's own E (16000 ksi) and G (6000 ksi). CDA publishes no direct value; the reported 0.34 should be treated as approximate.
Density: MakeItFrom lists 8.2 g/cm3 vs CDA/Concast/AZoM 8.53 g/cm3 (0.308 lb/in3); MakeItFrom appears to use its own rounded estimate and was disregarded for density.
Ultimate strength: MakeItFrom rounds H02 UTS to 420 MPa (61 ksi) vs CDA typical 62 ksi for 0.040 in flat product — minor rounding difference.

CDA web page table extraction: automated HTML-to-markdown conversion of alloys.copper.org dropped the H02 yield-strength cell, initially making the row look like YS 25 ksi / 70% elongation (physically implausible for half-hard). Resolved via MakeItFrom and supplier listings: actual CDA row is TS 62 / YS 52 / El 25 / HRB 70.

Spec minimums vs typicals: ASTM B927/B927M-23 H02 rod minimums (TS 57/54/50 ksi, YS 35/32/30 ksi by diameter, per Wieland Concast) are well below the flat-product typical values reported here — different product form and statistical basis; do not mix.
MatWeb hosts C26000 H02 datasheets but was not accessed per EULA restrictions; values reported here are consistent with what third parties quote from it.

rows.name: Nylon 6/6 (PA66), unfilled
rows.condition: Unfilled injection-molding resin (Celanese/DuPont Zytel 101L NC010), dry as molded (DAM), 23 C. Conditioned (50% RH) values noted per property — PA66 is strongly moisture-sensitive.

properties.source_citation: Celanese, Zytel 101L NC010 Nylon Resin Product Data Sheet, rev. 2023-04-04, 'Other properties' table, Density, ISO 1183, dry
properties.cross_check: DuPont Zytel 101L NC010 TDS rev. 2018-03-27 (plastore.it mirror): 1140 kg/m3 (ISO 1183, dry); Ensinger TECAMID 66 natural US datasheet: 1.14 g/cm3
properties.notes: Dry value. TDS also lists density of melt 970 kg/m3. Specific gravity equivalent 1.14.

properties.source_citation: Celanese, Zytel 101L NC010 Nylon Resin Product Data Sheet, rev. 2023-04-04, 'Typical mechanical properties' table, Tensile Modulus, ISO 527-1/-2, dry/cond = 3100/1400 MPa
properties.cross_check: DuPont Zytel 101L NC010 TDS rev. 2018-03-27 (plastore.it mirror): 3100/1400 MPa, ISO 527-1/-2
properties.notes: MOISTURE SENSITIVITY: drops from 3100 MPa dry-as-molded to 1400 MPa conditioned at 50% RH (more than half). Extruded stock shapes test higher (Ertalon 66 SA: 3550 MPa dry, ISO 527); Ensinger US ASTM D638 figure is 350,000 psi.

properties.source_citation: Celanese, Zytel 101L NC010 Nylon Resin Product Data Sheet, rev. 2023-04-04, Poisson's ratio, dry/cond = 0.37/0.43 (DuPont 2018 edition lists test standard ISO 527-1/-2)
properties.cross_check: DuPont Zytel 101L NC010 TDS rev. 2018-03-27 (plastore.it mirror): 0.37/0.43, ISO 527-1/-2
properties.notes: 0.37 dry as molded; rises to 0.43 conditioned at 50% RH. Generic literature values of ~0.39-0.41 for PA66 usually refer to partially conditioned material.

properties.source_citation: Celanese, Zytel 101L NC010 Nylon Resin Product Data Sheet, rev. 2023-04-04, Yield stress at 50 mm/min, ISO 527-1/-2, dry/cond = 82/55 MPa
properties.cross_check: DuPont Zytel 101L NC010 TDS rev. 2018-03-27 (plastore.it mirror): 82/55 MPa; Ensinger TECAMID 66 natural (extruded, ASTM D638): tensile strength at yield 12,000 psi at 73 F
properties.notes: Tensile yield stress (yield strain 4.5% dry). MOISTURE SENSITIVITY: 82 MPa dry-as-molded falls to 55 MPa conditioned at 50% RH. BASF Ultramid A3K (search excerpt, not directly loaded) lists 85/50 MPa — small grade-to-grade variation.

properties.source_citation: Ensinger TECAMID 66 natural (PA66 extruded stock shapes), US product datasheet, Tensile strength at break, ASTM D638, 73 F
properties.cross_check: Quadrant Ertalon 66 SA TDS (theplasticshop.co.uk): tensile strength 93 MPa dry, ISO 527-1/-2 (extruded rod); both are extruded-shape, dry-material figures
properties.notes: Molding-resin TDS (Zytel 101L) reports no break stress dry-as-molded — material is ductile (strain at break 45%), so yield stress (82 MPa) is the maximum engineering stress and the practical 'tensile strength' for molded DAM parts. This 12,300 psi break value is from extruded stock shape, dry; conditioned strength is substantially lower.

discrepancies: Moisture condition dominates all mechanical properties: dry-as-molded vs 50% RH conditioned values are 3100 vs 1400 MPa (modulus), 82 vs 55 MPa (yield), 0.37 vs 0.43 (Poisson). A single-number row is misleading without the DAM qualifier.

Tensile modulus varies by product form and test standard: 3100 MPa (Zytel 101L molded, ISO 527) vs 3550 MPa (Ertalon 66 SA extruded, ISO 527) vs 350,000 psi ~= 2.4 GPa (Ensinger US page, ASTM D638) — the Ensinger ASTM figure is notably lower than the ISO figures.
Dry yield strength ranges 82-90 MPa across unfilled PA66 grades (Zytel 101L 82 MPa, BASF Ultramid A3K 85 MPa per search excerpt, Ertalon 66 SA 90 MPa).

No break/ultimate stress is published for the dry-as-molded molding resin (ductile failure at 45% strain); ultimate_strength reported here is from an extruded-shape datasheet and is essentially equal to yield.
MatWeb not accessed per EULA restriction; all values verified via manufacturer/supplier TDS mirrors loaded directly.

rows.name: Douglas-fir (coast)
rows.condition: Clear, straight-grained small specimens at 12% moisture content; "Coast" Douglas-fir = grown in Oregon/Washington west of the Cascade Mountains summit (Wood Handbook Table 5-3 footnote e). Average values from species sampling — typical, not design minima.

properties.source_citation: USDA Forest Products Laboratory, Wood Handbook: Wood as an Engineering Material, FPL-GTR-282, Chapter 5 (Senalik & Farber), Table 5-3b, p. 5-12, Douglas-fir Coast, 12% MC
properties.cross_check: FPL-GTR-190 (2010 ed.), Chapter 5, Table 5-3b, p. 5-12: 0.48 (identical); also 0.48 in metric Table 5-3a of both editions. https://www.fpl.fs.usda.gov/documnts/fplgtr/fplgtr190/chapter_05.pdf
properties.notes: Reported as SPECIFIC GRAVITY, not density (pipeline must convert: footnote b states SG is based on ovendry weight and volume at the tabulated moisture condition, i.e. volume at 12% MC for this row). Approximate density at 12% MC is ~32-34 lb/ft3 but that number is not published in this table, so only SG is recorded.

properties.source_citation: USDA FPL Wood Handbook FPL-GTR-282, Chapter 5, Table 5-3b (Static bending, Modulus of elasticity), p. 5-12, Douglas-fir Coast, 12% MC
properties.cross_check: FPL-GTR-190 Table 5-3b, p. 5-12: 1.95 x10^6 lbf/in2 (identical); metric Table 5-3a in GTR-282 gives 13,400 MPa for the same row. https://www.fpl.fs.usda.gov/documnts/fplgtr/fplgtr190/chapter_05.pdf
properties.notes: E_L (longitudinal) from static bending: footnote c — measured on a simply supported, center-loaded beam, span-to-depth ratio 14/1; as tabulated it INCLUDES shear-deflection effect and may be increased ~10% to correct (i.e. ~2.15 x10^6 psi shear-free). Educational consumers comparing to axial E_L should note this.

properties.source_citation: USDA FPL Wood Handbook FPL-GTR-282, Chapter 5, Table 5-2 (Poisson's ratios for various species at approximately 12% moisture content), p. 5-3, Douglas-fir, mu_LR
properties.cross_check: FPL-GTR-190 Table 5-2, p. 5-3: mu_LR = 0.292 (identical). https://www.fpl.fs.usda.gov/documnts/fplgtr/fplgtr190/chapter_05.pdf
properties.notes: Wood is orthotropic — six Poisson's ratios. Value given is mu_LR; full Douglas-fir set in Table 5-2: mu_LR 0.292, mu_LT 0.449, mu_RT 0.390, mu_TR 0.374, mu_RL 0.036, mu_TL 0.029. Table 5-2 lists 'Douglas-fir' at species level, NOT subdivided coast/interior. Shear-modulus ratios in Table 5-1: G_LR/E_L = 0.064, G_LT/E_L = 0.078, G_RT/E_L = 0.007.

properties.source_citation: USDA FPL Wood Handbook FPL-GTR-282, Chapter 5, Table 5-3b (Static bending, Modulus of rupture), p. 5-12, Douglas-fir Coast, 12% MC
properties.cross_check: FPL-GTR-190 Table 5-3b, p. 5-12: 12,400 lbf/in2 (identical); metric Table 5-3a in GTR-282 gives 85,000 kPa for the same row. https://www.fpl.fs.usda.gov/documnts/fplgtr/fplgtr190/chapter_05.pdf
properties.notes: Average MOR of small clear specimens — NOT a design value; visually/mechanically graded lumber design values (e.g. NDS Fb) are far lower. Handbook notes MOR is not a true stress (flexure formula valid only to elastic limit).

properties.source_citation: USDA FPL Wood Handbook FPL-GTR-282, Chapter 5, Table 5-3b (Compression parallel to grain), p. 5-12, Douglas-fir Coast, 12% MC
properties.cross_check: FPL-GTR-190 Table 5-3b, p. 5-12: 7,230 lbf/in2 (identical); metric Table 5-3a in GTR-282 gives 49,900 kPa. https://www.fpl.fs.usda.gov/documnts/fplgtr/fplgtr190/chapter_05.pdf
properties.notes: Maximum crushing strength parallel to grain (footnote a). Included as a bonus beyond the requested wood property set since it is a primary wood strength property. Compression perpendicular to grain (fiber stress at proportional limit) for the same row is 800 lbf/in2.

discrepancies: No discrepancies: FPL-GTR-282 (current edition) and FPL-GTR-190 (2010 edition) publish identical values for Douglas-fir Coast at 12% MC (SG 0.48; MOR 12,400 lbf/in2; MOE 1.95 x10^6 lbf/in2; compression parallel 7,230 lbf/in2) and identical Poisson's ratios.

Yield strength and ultimate (tensile) strength were intentionally omitted: the Wood Handbook does not tabulate a yield strength for wood, and tension-parallel-to-grain data are sparse; MOR and compression parallel are the standard strength descriptors per the assignment.

Caveat rather than discrepancy: tabulated bending MOE includes a shear-deflection effect; the handbook says it may be increased ~10% to approximate shear-free E_L. Third-party tables sometimes quote the +10% corrected value (~2.1-2.2 Msi), which can look like a disagreement.
Poisson's ratios and elastic ratios (Tables 5-1, 5-2) are for Douglas-fir at species level, not specific to the Coast variety, unlike the Table 5-3 strength rows.

rows.name: Normal-weight concrete, f'c = 4000 psi class
rows.condition: Normal-weight (plain), specified 28-day cylinder compressive strength f'c = 4000 psi, moist-cured per standard practice

properties.source_citation: University of Memphis CIVL 1101/1112 course notes, 'Properties of Concrete' (Concrete Material Properties slides, p. 6/10): reinforced concrete with normal aggregates is about 150 lb/ft3 (pcf); if 5 pcf is allowed for steel, plain concrete w = 145 pcf
properties.cross_check: Penn State AE thesis design tables (engr.psu.edu/ae/thesis/portfolios/2010/glc111/midterm/lw_conc_properties.pdf) lists '145 pcf (normal-weight)' explicitly; loaded and confirmed
properties.notes: 145 pcf is plain normal-weight concrete; 150 pcf is the standard design value for REINFORCED concrete (ASCE 7 convention). Pipeline should treat 145 pcf as the plain-material density. WSDOT uses higher agency values (see discrepancies).

properties.source_citation: f'c = specified 28-day cylinder compressive strength per ACI 318 class designation (ACI 318 is paywalled; not fetched). University of Memphis CIVL 1101/1112 notes p. 5/10: 'Most structural concrete have f'c values in the 3,000 to 5,000 psi range'; compressive strength defined as measured maximum resistance at age 28 days
properties.cross_check: ConcreteNetwork 'What is Concrete PSI?' (concretenetwork.com/concrete/compressive-strength-psi.html): cylinders tested 28 days after casting; 3000-4000 psi typical for slabs/driveways, 4000 psi mixes discussed; loaded and confirmed
properties.notes: This is the class-defining specified strength, not a measured typical. ACI 318 requires the actual mix to be proportioned so average strength exceeds f'c; individual test acceptance criteria are in ACI 318 Ch. 26.

properties.source_citation: ACI 318 elastic modulus formula for normal-weight concrete, Ec = 57,000*sqrt(f'c) psi (ACI 318-08 Sec. 8.5.1; ACI 318-19 Eq. 19.2.2.1.b), as quoted with worked example by CivilEngPro, 'Modulus of Elasticity of Concrete'; ACI 318 itself is paywalled and was not fetched
properties.cross_check: U. of Memphis CIVL 1101 notes p. 6/10 derive E = 57,000*sqrt(f'c) from E = 33*w^1.5*sqrt(f'c) at w = 145 pcf (ce.memphis.edu PDF, loaded); Penn State table (engr.psu.edu PDF, loaded) gives Ec = 3644 ksi at f'c = 4000 psi using the full ACI 33*wc^1.5*sqrt(f'c) formula at 145 pcf, within ~1% of 3605 ksi
properties.notes: Computed from the ACI 318 formula Ec = 57,000*sqrt(f'c) with f'c = 4000 psi: exact arithmetic gives 3,604,997 psi, conventionally rounded to 3,605,000 psi (3,605 ksi, ~3.6 Msi). This is a code estimate of the secant modulus, not a specified minimum; measured values vary with aggregate stiffness.

properties.source_citation: Gilson Company blog, 'Modulus of Elasticity & Poisson's Ratio of Concrete: Beyond Compressive Strength' (re ASTM C469 testing): 'typical ranges of Poisson's ratio values for concrete are 0.15 to 0.20 for normal weight concrete'
properties.cross_check: CivilEngPro modulus-of-elasticity page (civilengpro.com, loaded): 'for concrete under small stresses, Poisson's ratio ranges approximately 0.15 to 0.20'
properties.notes: Published as a typical RANGE 0.15-0.20 for normal-weight concrete; 0.20 recorded here as the commonly assumed design value (e.g., AASHTO LRFD uses 0.2). Use the range, not a hard single value, in educational display.

discrepancies: Unit weight: WSDOT Bridge Design Memo 05-2010 (wsdot.wa.gov/eesc/bridge/designmemos/05-2010.htm, loaded) directs 150 pcf for plain and 155 pcf for reinforced normal-weight concrete for dead-load calcs - 5 pcf above the common textbook/ASCE 7 values of 145 (plain) / 150 (reinforced) pcf; agency-specific conservatism, not an error.

Young's modulus: ACI 318's full formula Ec = 33*wc^1.5*sqrt(f'c) at wc = 145 pcf gives ~3,644,000 psi (Penn State table) vs the simplified Ec = 57,000*sqrt(f'c) giving ~3,605,000 psi (~1% lower, since 33*145^1.5 = 57,600 is rounded down to 57,000 in the code); both are ACI-sanctioned.
Poisson's ratio is published as a range (0.15-0.20) rather than a single value; 0.2 was recorded by convention.

Engineering ToolBox and CivilToday pages returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch and were not used; MatWeb was not accessed per policy. ACI 318 and ASTM C469 are paywalled and cited by designation only, with numbers verified via the accessible secondary sources listed.

cost_classes.rationale: Commodity carbon structural steel — the cheapest structural metal. USGS MCS 2026 (Feb 2026, loaded): iron described as 'the least expensive and most widely used metal'; 2025e US raw steel 82 Mt with ~$149B sales value across mill products; HRC/structural shapes trade around $0.35-0.50/lb, roughly 1/4 the price of aluminum ingot.
Plain medium-carbon bar steel; small grade premium over A36 but same commodity-carbon-steel price class (USGS MCS 2026 iron/steel anchor, loaded).

Ni-Cr-Mo alloying plus aerospace-quality consumable-electrode vacuum melting (AMS 6414) typically multiplies price several-fold over commodity carbon steel (knowledge-based premium on the verified USGS low-cost steel anchor); still far below titanium. Commodity-grade 4340 sits at the bottom of medium; CEVM/VAR aerospace stock trends toward high.

USGS MCS 2026 (loaded): primary aluminum ingot average US market price 180 cents/lb 2025e (+39% YoY) — roughly 4x carbon steel per pound; 6061 is the commodity wrought alloy with the smallest alloy premium.
Same 180 cents/lb 2025e USGS ingot anchor; 7075 aerospace sheet/plate carries a meaningful conversion premium over 6061 (top of the medium band, knowledge-based premium).
Same USGS aluminum anchor (180 cents/lb ingot 2025e); 2024-T3 aerospace-spec sheet premium comparable to 7075 — top of medium.

USGS MCS 2026 (loaded): titanium sponge $12/kg 2025e (~$5.40/lb raw metal, ~3x aluminum ingot, >10x steel) with US 100% net import reliance; alloyed Ti-6Al-4V mill products (melt + convert) historically run an order of magnitude above sponge, the most expensive material in this set by far.

Carbon-steel base (USGS low anchor, loaded) plus ~18% Cr / 8% Ni surcharge puts 304 sheet at roughly 3-4x carbon steel, comparable per-pound to aluminum semi-products. USGS MCS 2026 publishes no stainless unit price; relative placement is knowledge-based on the verified steel and nickel-bearing-alloy spreads.
Gray iron castings are the lowest-cost ferrous engineering product; USGS MCS 2026 (loaded): iron is 'the least expensive and most widely used metal'.

USGS MCS 2026 (loaded): COMEX copper averaged a record-high $4.80/lb 2025e (US producer cathode $4.90/lb) — ~2.7x aluminum ingot and >10x steel; C26000 is ~70% Cu and brass mill strip prices sit above the copper content, clearly above the aluminum/stainless tier but below titanium.

Not a USGS commodity; no price source loaded this session (honesty note). Handbook/market knowledge: unfilled PA66 resin historically ~$1.30-2.50/lb — per-pound comparable to aluminum ingot (medium), though cheap per part by volume given 1.14 g/cc density.

Not a USGS commodity; no price source loaded this session (honesty note). Commodity softwood framing lumber (~$400-600/MBF in recent years, i.e. cents per pound) is among the cheapest structural materials per unit strength.

SECONDARY CAUTIONS FOR THE PIPELINE: (a) 1045 normalized UTS 640 MPa is single-primary-source (conflicting 540 MPa reproductions exist; 640 is the hardness-consistent choice) — store with a caution flag or as a 570-700 MPa typical band; (b) gray iron E must be stored as the 13.0-16.4 Msi SECANT range, never the 13 Msi point; (c) concrete Poisson 0.2 should display the 0.15-0.20 range; (d) the 304 triple (E 28e3 ksi, G 11.2e3 ksi, nu 0.29) is mutually inconsistent at ~5% — never derive one constant from the other two; (e) PA66 values are meaningless without the dry-as-molded qualifier (modulus halves at 50% RH); (f) Douglas-fir 'density' is specific gravity and the bending MOE includes shear deflection (+~10% to shear-free) — both correctly documented, must survive into display.

COST BASIS: relative classes anchored to USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries 2026 (February 2026), 2025 estimated prices verified by loading the chapter PDFs: aluminum ingot 180 c/lb; copper COMEX $4.80/lb (record high); titanium sponge $12/kg (US 100% import reliant, zero domestic production); iron/steel chapter publishes no unit price (82 Mt raw steel, ~$149B sales value; 'iron is the least expensive and most widely used metal'). Resulting ladder: concrete/wood/gray iron/carbon steels = low; aluminum alloys/304/4340/PA66 = medium; brass (copper-bearing) = high; Ti-6Al-4V = very_high.
